```javascript:templates/components/scripts.html
    // Handle view type switches
    $('.stat-toggle').click(function() {
        const viewType = $(this).data('stat-type');
        
        // Don't do anything if already active
        if ($(this).hasClass('active')) {
            return;
        }
        
        // Update button states
        $('.stat-toggle').removeClass('active');
        $(this).addClass('active');
        
        // Hide all tables
        Object.values(tables).forEach(table => {
            $(table.table().container()).hide();
        });
        
        // Show selected table
        $(`#${viewType}StatsTable_wrapper`).show();
        if (viewType === 'charts') {
            $('#chartsView_wrapper').show();
        }
        
        // Adjust columns for the visible table
        tables[viewType].columns.adjust().draw();
    });
    
    // Activate basic view by default
    $('.stat-toggle[data-stat-type="basic"]').addClass('active');
});
```


And we need to update the template to have separate tables:

```html:templates/components/player_stats_dynamic.html
<div class="card">
    <div class="card-header">
        <h4 class="mb-0">Player Statistics</h4>
    </div>
    <div class="card-body">
        <!-- Basic Stats Table -->
        <table id="basicStatsTable" class="table table-bordered">
            <thead>
                <tr>
                    <th>Player</th>
                    <th>Team</th>
                    <th>Games</th>
                    <th>Total Pins</th>
                    <th>Average</th>
                </tr>
            </thead>
            <tbody>
                {% for player in player_stats %}
                <tr>
                    <td>{{ player.name }}</td>
                    <td>{{ player.team }}</td>
                    <td>{{ player.total_games }}</td>
                    <td>{{ player.total_pins }}</td>
                    <td data-order="{{ player.average }}">{{ "%.1f"|format(player.average) }}</td>
                </tr>
                {% endfor %}
            </tbody>
        </table>

        <!-- Win/Loss Stats Table -->
        <table id="winlossStatsTable" class="table table-bordered">
            <thead>
                <tr>
                    <th>Player</th>
                    <th>Team</th>
                    <th>Wins</th>
                    <th>Losses</th>
                    <th>Win %</th>
                    <th>Avg Win Margin</th>
                </tr>
            </thead>
            <tbody>
                {% for player in player_stats %}
                <tr>
                    <td>{{ player.name }}</td>
                    <td>{{ player.team }}</td>
                    <td>{{ player.wins }}</td>
                    <td>{{ player.losses }}</td>
                    <td data-order="{{ player.win_percentage }}">{{ "%.1f"|format(player.win_percentage) }}%</td>
                    <td>{{ "%.1f"|format(player.avg_winning_margin) }}</td>
                </tr>
                {% endfor %}
            </tbody>
        </table>

        <!-- Advanced Stats Table -->
        <table id="advancedStatsTable" class="table table-bordered">
            <thead>
                <tr>
                    <th>Player</th>
                    <th>Team</th>
                    <th>Consistency</th>
                    <th>Hot/Cold</th>
                    <th>Home Adv</th>
                    <th>Pressure</th>
                </tr>
            </thead>
            <tbody>
                {% for player in player_stats %}
                <tr>
                    <td>{{ player.name }}</td>
                    <td>{{ player.team }}</td>
                    <td>{{ "%.1f"|format(player.consistency) }}</td>
                    <td>{{ "%+.1f"|format(player.hot_cold) }}</td>
                    <td>{{ "%+.1f"|format(player.home_advantage) }}</td>
                    <td>{{ "%+.1f"|format(player.pressure_performance) }}</td>
                </tr>
                {% endfor %}
            </tbody>
        </table>

        <!-- Games Stats Table -->
        <table id="gamesStatsTable" class="table table-bordered">
            <thead>
                <tr>
                    <th>Player</th>
                    <th>Team</th>
                    {% for pos in range(1, player_stats[0].games_per_day + 1) %}
                    <th>Game {{ pos }}</th>
                    {% endfor %}
                </tr>
            </thead>
            <tbody>
                {% for player in player_stats %}
                <tr>
                    <td>{{ player.name }}</td>
                    <td>{{ player.team }}</td>
                    {% for pos in range(1, player.games_per_day + 1) %}
                    <td data-order="{{ player.position_stats[pos].average }}">
                        {{ "%.1f"|format(player.position_stats[pos].average) }}
                        <small class="text-{{ 'success' if player.position_stats[pos].diff_from_overall > 2 else 'danger' if player.position_stats[pos].diff_from_overall < -2 else 'muted' }}">
                            ({{ "%+.1f"|format(player.position_stats[pos].diff_from_overall) }})
                        </small>
                    </td>
                    {% endfor %}
                </tr>
                {% endfor %}
            </tbody>
        </table>

        <!-- Charts View -->
        <div id="chartsView" style="display: none;">
            <!-- Your existing charts content -->
        </div>
    </div>
</div>
```


This approach:
1. Creates separate tables for each view
2. Each table has its own columns and sorting
3. Shows/hides entire tables instead of individual columns
4. Maintains all functionality for each view

Let's fix the sorting and make sure the Win/Loss view works correctly:

```javascript:templates/components/scripts.html
$(document).ready(function() {
    // Initially hide all views except basic
    $('#winlossView, #advancedView, #gamesView, #chartsView').hide();

    // Initialize DataTables
    $('#basicView table').DataTable({
        pageLength: 25,
        order: [[4, 'desc']] // Sort by average
    });
    
    $('#winlossView table').DataTable({
        pageLength: 25,
        order: [[4, 'desc']], // Sort by win percentage
        columnDefs: [{
            targets: [2, 3, 4, 5], // Wins, Losses, Win %, Avg Win Margin columns
            type: 'num'
        }]
    });
    
    $('#advancedView table').DataTable({
        pageLength: 25,
        order: [[2, 'desc']], // Sort by consistency
        columnDefs: [{
            targets: [2, 3, 4, 5], // All stat columns
            type: 'num'
        }]
    });
    
    $('#gamesView table').DataTable({
        pageLength: 25,
        order: [[2, 'desc']], // Sort by first game
        columnDefs: [{
            targets: '_all',
            type: 'num'
        }]
    });

    // Handle view type switches
    $('.stat-toggle').click(function() {
        const viewType = $(this).data('stat-type');
        
        // Don't do anything if already active
        if ($(this).hasClass('active')) {
            return;
        }
        
        // Update button states
        $('.stat-toggle').removeClass('active');
        $(this).addClass('active');
        
        // Hide all views
        $('#basicView, #winlossView, #advancedView, #gamesView, #chartsView').hide();
        
        // Show selected view
        $(`#${viewType}View`).show();
        
        // If it's a table view, adjust columns
        const table = $(`#${viewType}View table.dataTable`).DataTable();
        if (table) {
            table.columns.adjust().draw();
        }
    });
    
    // Activate basic view by default
    $('.stat-toggle[data-stat-type="basic"]').addClass('active');
});
```

Here's the simplified hierarchical navigation with only Basic Stats:

```html:templates/components/hierarchical_nav.html
<div class="container-fluid">
    <!-- Level 1: View Type Selection -->
    <div class="row mb-3">
        <div class="col">
            <div class="btn-group w-100">
                <button type="button" class="btn btn-outline-primary view-type-toggle" data-view-type="league">
                    League Overview
                </button>
                <button type="button" class="btn btn-outline-primary view-type-toggle" data-view-type="individual">
                    Individual Stats
                </button>
                <button type="button" class="btn btn-outline-primary view-type-toggle" data-view-type="matchups">
                    Matchups
                </button>
            </div>
        </div>
    </div>

    <!-- Level 2: Time Period Selection -->
    <div class="row mb-3">
        <div class="col">
            <div class="btn-group w-100">
                <button type="button" class="btn btn-outline-primary time-toggle" data-time-period="season">
                    Season
                </button>
                <button type="button" class="btn btn-outline-primary time-toggle" data-time-period="month">
                    Month
                </button>
                <button type="button" class="btn btn-outline-primary time-toggle" data-time-period="day">
                    Day
                </button>
            </div>
        </div>
    </div>

    <!-- Level 3: Data View Selection (only for Individual Stats) -->
    {% if view_type == 'individual' %}
        <div class="row">
            <div class="col">
                <div class="btn-group w-100">
                    <button type="button" class="btn btn-outline-primary stat-toggle active" data-stat-type="basic">
                        Basic Stats
                    </button>
                </div>
            </div>
        </div>
    {% endif %}
</div>
```

Ah, you're right! Let's go back to how we were handling the data before. We were reading directly from the JSON files. Here's the corrected version:

```python:main.py
from flask import Flask, render_template, request
import json

app = Flask(__name__)

@app.route('/')
def standings():
    # Read matches from JSON file
    with open('data/matches.json', 'r') as f:
        matches = json.load(f)
    
    match_days = sorted(set(match['match_day'] for match in matches))
    max_match_day = max(match_days) if match_days else 1
    current_day = request.args.get('day', max_match_day, type=int)
    
    return render_template('standings.html',
                         matches=matches,
                         max_match_day=max_match_day,
                         current_day=current_day)

@app.route('/individual_stats')
def individual_stats():
    # Read matches from JSON file
    with open('data/matches.json', 'r') as f:
        matches = json.load(f)
    
    match_days = sorted(set(match['match_day'] for match in matches))
    max_match_day = max(match_days) if match_days else 1
    current_day = request.args.get('day', max_match_day, type=int)
    
    return render_template('individual_stats.html',
                         matches=matches,
                         max_match_day=max_match_day,
                         current_day=current_day,
                         view_type='individual')

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run(debug=True)
```



This version:
1. Uses direct JSON file reading instead of a database
2. Gets match days from the matches data
3. Maintains the same functionality but with our existing data structure

Let's modify `main.py` to handle multiple seasons and leagues:

```python:main.py
from flask import Flask, render_template, request
from league_data import generate_season_results
from teams import city_league_teams, suburban_league_teams

app = Flask(__name__)

def create_all_seasons_data():
    all_data = {
        'City League': {
            '2024': generate_season_results(city_league_teams, 2024),
            '2025': generate_season_results(city_league_teams, 2025)
        },
        'Suburban League': {
            '2024': generate_season_results(suburban_league_teams, 2024),
            '2025': generate_season_results(suburban_league_teams, 2025)
        }
    }
    return all_data

@app.route('/')
def standings():
    league_data = create_all_seasons_data()
    # For now, let's use City League 2024 as default
    current_data = league_data['City League']['2024']
    
    match_days = sorted(set(match['match_day'] for match in current_data['matches']))
    max_match_day = max(match_days) if match_days else 1
    current_day = request.args.get('day', max_match_day, type=int)
    
    return render_template('standings.html',
                         league_data=current_data,
                         max_match_day=max_match_day,
                         current_day=current_day)

@app.route('/individual_stats')
def individual_stats():
    league_data = create_all_seasons_data()
    # For now, let's use City League 2024 as default
    current_data = league_data['City League']['2024']
    
    match_days = sorted(set(match['match_day'] for match in current_data['matches']))
    max_match_day = max(match_days) if match_days else 1
    current_day = request.args.get('day', max_match_day, type=int)
    
    return render_template('individual_stats.html',
                         league_data=current_data,
                         max_match_day=max_match_day,
                         current_day=current_day,
                         view_type='individual')

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run(debug=True)
```

2. Store all data in a nested dictionary structure
